scope.title: 5T Scope
scope.description: Define the PFMEA team, timeframe, tools, tasks, and trend data sources.

structure.title: Structure Analysis
structure.description: Break down Process Item → Process Step → Work Element (4M).

function.title: Function Analysis
function.description: Define functions for each structure node, build a 3-level function tree, and distinguish product/process characteristics.

failure.title: Failure Analysis
failure.description: Build failure chains for each process step function. Causes are organized by 4M.

risk.title: Risk Analysis
risk.description: Evaluate three-tier severity (plant / customer / end user, max value), occurrence, detection, and calculate AP.
risk.missingControlHint: Please fill in prevention/detection controls first
risk.controlsFirst: Rate O/D after controls are filled

confirm.title: Result Documentation
confirm.description: Confirm the PFMEA analysis results and complete creation.

page.title: PFMEA Wizard
page.step1Incomplete: Structure analysis incomplete: process steps need process numbers and work elements need 4M classification
page.step2Incomplete: Function analysis incomplete: all work elements need functions and the 3-level function chain must be complete
page.step3Incomplete: Failure analysis incomplete: all process step functions need named failure chains and controls
page.step4Incomplete: Risk analysis incomplete: three-tier severity, O/D must be filled and controls must not be empty
page.step5Incomplete: Optimization incomplete: AP=H rows need responsible person and planned completion date
page.conflictContent: The document has been modified by someone else, please refresh and retry.

step0.title: 5T Scope
step0.points: The 5Ts are Team, Timing, Tool, Task, Trend; use them to frame the scope of this process analysis.

fields.name: Team
fields.desc: Cross-functional core team: process/manufacturing engineers, quality engineers, equipment maintenance, production leads, purchasing/supplier reps, and customer reps.

fields.name: Timing
fields.desc: PFMEA start and end dates; launch early in process design and complete major optimization before pilot production.

fields.name: Tool
fields.desc: Process flow diagram, P-Diagram, fishbone (4M), PFMEA template, control plan draft, historical lessons-learned library.

fields.name: Task
fields.desc: Process scope, production line, operation range, and special characteristics to focus on.

fields.name: Trend
fields.desc: Historical PFMEA, process SPC data, NCRs, customer complaints, CAPA, rework/scrap records, and audit findings.

step0.example: Team: SMT process & quality; Timing: Q2 2026; Tools: process flow + 4M fishbone; Task: SMT welding OP10-OP40; Trend: 6-month placement defect trend

step1.title: Structure Analysis
step1.points: Decompose the process into Process Item → Process Step → Work Element (4M). Process steps need operation numbers and work elements need 4M classification.

fields.name: Process No. OPxx
fields.desc: Operation number such as OP10, OP20, used to uniquely identify the operation sequence.

fields.name: 4M classification
fields.desc: Classify work elements by Man, Machine, Material, and Environment to support failure-cause analysis.

step1.example: OP10 placement / Machine: high-speed chip mounter / Material: solder paste

step2.title: Function Analysis
step2.points: Build a 3-level function tree, distinguish product characteristics from process characteristics, and maintain CC/SC special characteristics.

fields.name: Function name
fields.desc: Description of what the process item, step, or work element must accomplish.

fields.name: Product characteristic / specification
fields.desc: Product quality characteristic and specification limits influenced by the process step.

fields.name: Process characteristic
fields.desc: Directly monitorable process parameters such as temperature, pressure, or speed.

fields.name: CC/SC
fields.desc: Critical characteristic (CC) or significant characteristic (SC) flag.

step2.example: Accurate placement / offset ≤ 0.05mm / placement pressure 0.5±0.05 MPa / CC

step3.title: Failure Analysis
step3.points: Attach failure modes to process-step functions, organize failure causes by 4M, and build the failure mode-effect-cause-control chain.

fields.name: FM Failure Mode
fields.desc: Form in which the process function is not met.

fields.name: FE Failure Effect
fields.desc: Effect of the failure mode on downstream operations, the customer, or regulation.

fields.name: FC Failure Cause
fields.desc: 4M cause of the failure mode, such as operator error, equipment wear, or material variation.

fields.name: PC/DC
fields.desc: Existing prevention controls (PC) and detection controls (DC).

step3.example: Placement offset (FM) / downstream cannot weld (FE) / nozzle wear (Machine) (FC)

step4.title: Risk Analysis
step4.points: Use the maximum of three-tier severity (plant / customer / end user), evaluate occurrence and detection, then calculate AP.

fields.name: S (Plant / Customer / End User)
fields.desc: Rate severity for each tier, then take the maximum value.

fields.name: O Occurrence
fields.desc: Likelihood that the failure cause will occur.

fields.name: D Detection
fields.desc: Ability of current controls to detect the failure cause or mode.

fields.name: AP
fields.desc: Action Priority derived from S/O/D lookup as H/M/L.

fields.name: Class
fields.desc: Inherited special-characteristic flag (read-only).

step5.title: Optimization
step5.points: AP=H rows have priority. Define optimization measures, responsible person, planned completion date, and re-evaluate S'/O'/D'/AP.

fields.name: Optimization measure
fields.desc: Specific action taken to reduce risk.

fields.name: Responsible person
fields.desc: Owner of the measure.

fields.name: Planned completion date
fields.desc: Target completion date for the measure.

fields.name: Status
fields.desc: Open, planned, done, not executed, etc.

fields.name: Re-evaluation S'/O'/D'/AP
fields.desc: Re-assess risk after the measure is implemented.

step6.title: Result Documentation
step6.points: Check the summary statistics and complete the PFMEA creation.
